meta.contentVersion: DSM-5-TR / ICD-11 aide-memoire dataset with Australian coding-context note
meta.scope: Quick-reference aide-memoire for diagnostic specifiers and clinical notes. It is not a diagnostic instrument, substitute manual, treatment guide, billing/coding authority, or Australian ICD-11 implementation guide.
meta.disclaimer: Use with formal DSM-5-TR materials, current WHO ICD-11 materials, Australian ICD-10-AM/ACHI/ACS requirements where relevant, local governance, and qualified clinical judgement.
meta.scopeWarning: This is diagnostic specifier aide-memoire content, not psychotherapy, treatment, prescribing, billing, or diagnostic-manual content.

items.label: With/without impairment in comprehension of language

With impairment in reading (word reading accuracy, reading rate or fluency, reading comprehension)

items.label: With delayed expression (full criteria not met until at least 6 months after the event)
items.meaning: For Post-Traumatic Stress Disorder, "With delayed expression (full criteria not met until at least 6 months after the event)" uses the stated threshold: full criteria not met until at least 6 months after the event.

items.label: With prominent negative alterations in cognition and mood
With disturbances in self-organisation (affective dysregulation, negative self-concept, disturbances in relationships)
items.meaning: ICD-11 complex PTSD domain covering emotional regulation difficulty, persistent negative self-beliefs, and relationship disturbance.

items.label: Current severity
items.meaning: For Complex PTSD (ICD-11), current severity refers to current burden across PTSD symptom burden plus affect dysregulation, negative self-concept, relationship disturbance, impairment, and safety concerns.

items.label: With mixed anxiety and depressed mood
With mixed disturbance of emotions and conduct

items.label: With inadequate food intake associated with limited availability
items.meaning: Inadequate intake is linked to limited food availability rather than body-image concern.

items.label: With avoidance/restriction based on sensory characteristics
items.meaning: Food avoidance is driven by sensory features such as taste, texture, smell, temperature, or appearance.

items.label: With avoidance related to aversive consequences of eating
items.meaning: Food avoidance is driven by feared consequences such as choking, vomiting, pain, or allergic reaction.

items.label: Level of personality functioning (0 little/no, 1 some, 2 moderate, 3 severe, 4 extreme)
Negative affectivity
items.meaning: Trait pattern involving frequent, intense, or poorly regulated negative emotions such as anxiety, anger, shame, or emotional lability.

items.label: Detachment
items.meaning: Trait pattern involving social withdrawal, emotional restriction, limited intimacy, or reduced engagement with others.

items.label: Disinhibition
items.meaning: Trait pattern involving impulsivity, poor planning, distractibility, or risk-prone behaviour.

items.label: Personality Difficulty (subthreshold)
items.meaning: ICD-11 descriptor for clinically relevant personality problems below the threshold for personality disorder.

items.label: Mild
items.meaning: For ICD-11 Dimensional Model, mild refers to current burden across self-functioning, interpersonal functioning, pervasiveness, distress, risk, and social or occupational impairment.

items.label: Moderate
items.meaning: For ICD-11 Dimensional Model, moderate refers to current burden across self-functioning, interpersonal functioning, pervasiveness, distress, risk, and social or occupational impairment.

items.label: Severe
items.meaning: For ICD-11 Dimensional Model, severe refers to current burden across self-functioning, interpersonal functioning, pervasiveness, distress, risk, and social or occupational impairment.

items.label: Negative affectivity, detachment, dissociality, disinhibition, anankastia
items.meaning: ICD-11 personality trait qualifiers used to describe the dominant style of personality disturbance.

items.label: Borderline pattern qualifier
items.meaning: ICD-11 qualifier used when the personality-disorder presentation has a prominent borderline-pattern profile.

items.label: Sexually aroused by exposing genitals to prepubertal children
Predominantly online
items.meaning: Gaming behaviour mainly occurs online or offline.

items.label: Predominantly offline
items.meaning: Gaming behaviour mainly occurs online or offline.

items.label: Current severity
items.meaning: For Gaming Disorder, current severity refers to current burden across loss of control, persistence, craving or preoccupation, harms, impairment, and relapse/remission status.

items.label: Affective dysregulation, negative self-concept, disturbances in relationships
items.meaning: ICD-11 complex PTSD domain covering emotional regulation difficulty, persistent negative self-beliefs, and relationship disturbance.

items.label: Induced by psychoactive substances including medications
items.meaning: Symptoms are classified as substance- or medication-induced in the ICD-11 context.

items.label: Secondary to a medical condition not classified elsewhere
items.meaning: The mental or behavioural presentation is classified as secondary to another medical condition.

items.label: With/without possession
items.meaning: The dissociative presentation includes experiences of being taken over by an external identity, spirit, or power.

items.label: Reclassified to conditions related to sexual health (not mental disorders)
items.meaning: ICD-11 places gender incongruence outside the mental disorders chapter.
